title: Water Main Pressure Surge Detector and Slow-Close Valve Controller
description: Monitors a pressurised water main at 1 kHz to detect water hammer transients, logs surge waveforms to SD, computes predicted surge pressure from Joukowsky equation, and adaptively adjusts the close time of a motorised ball valve to keep ΔP ≤ 200 kPa — targeting buried pipe infrastructure rather than surface water quality or harvesting.
objective: Detect water hammer events >50 kPa, log waveform timestamps to SD, enforce slow-close time that limits Joukowsky surge to ≤200 kPa on a 100 mm steel main at 2 m/s flow.
constraints: Arduino Uno, MPX5700AP sensor, motorised ball valve, relay module, SD card; 100 mm steel main, operating pressure 350 kPa, flow velocity 2 m/s; 12 V DC supply.
supporting_fact_titles: Joukowsky Water Hammer Pressure Surge in Pipe Flow
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.MPX5700AP Pipe Pressure Transducer: Sampled at 1 kHz; peak detection algorithm identifies surge events above 50 kPa baseline noise.
element_usage_notes.Slow-Close Motorised Ball Valve: Close time proportionally extended by PID based on measured previous surge vs. 200 kPa limit.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Inner loop: close-rate setpoint tracking; outer loop: measured peak surge vs. 200 kPa setpoint adjusts close time.
element_usage_notes.Buzzer and LED Fault Annunciator: Alarm on surge >200 kPa; second alarm on sudden pressure drop >100 kPa (pipe burst indicator).

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Peak surge pressure from each event drives outer PID loop to adjust future valve close time.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output incrementally adjusts valve close time to reduce surge on next operation.
Surge >200 kPa or burst-drop >100 kPa directly triggers alarm independent of PID.
